violations.description: Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment. -Employee with the serve safe certificate is no longer working at the establishment.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Initial testing of the Sanitation bucket on site revealed a less than 100ppm Quat concentration. The staff on duty fixed the issue and the new bucket tested to adequate levels.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Cooked Chicken breast and eggs are not date marked in the large cooler/prep table.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. -The doors and floor of the front large cooler are dirty. The floor of the smaller cooler/milk cooler needs cleaned.

Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. -Commercial sandwich press does not have a ventilation hood system provided.

Linen storage. — --Clean linen was not stored in a clean, dry location or protected from contamination. -Bag of clean linen found on the floor of a small utility/mop room next to a water heater with chemicals stored on a shelf above. Advise raising off the floor.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Attachments to walls and ceilings (e.g. light fixtures, vent covers, decorations etc..) were not cleanable and/or maintained in good repair. -The small ceiling vent cover above sandwich press is covered in oil/grease and should be removed and cleaned.
Food supplies. — --Food item was adulterated, contaminated or unsafe for human consumption. Cooked chicken breast in prep table cooler is spoiled and should be discarded.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Chicken Breast and soups in front cold-holding station not label and dated.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. Commercial sandwich hot press not under a hood system.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Reach in upright and prep table cooler have a buildup of debris and need cleaning.
Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Tested the sanitation concentration and found below acceptable levels.
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
